name: 09_Recommendation_System_NCF_MovieLens_ML
query: Nisaidie kuunda mfumo wa kupendekeza filamu kulingana na alama za watumiaji kutoka kwenye dataset ya MovieLens kwa kutumia mbinu ya Neural Collaborative Filtering (NCF). Kwanza, pakia dataset na igawanye katika seti za mafunzo na majaribio katika `src/data_loader.py`. Kisha, tekeleza mbinu ya NCF na msingi wa matrix factorization katika `src/model.py`. Kwa kutumia hizi, chapisha mfano wa mapendekezo 10 bora kwa mtumiaji wa majaribio kwa mbinu ya NCF na msingi na uhifadhi katika `results/metrics/top_10_recommendations.txt`. Itakuwa vizuri ikiwa mapendekezo haya ya sampuli yatakuwa na maana kulingana na mtumiaji wa majaribio. Pima utendaji wa mfumo kwa kutumia RMSE, MAE, nk., na uhifadhi matokeo ya tathmini hii katika `results/metrics/evaluation_metrics.txt`. Jaribu kuhakikisha kwamba kuna utunzaji wa njia thabiti ambao unaweza kushughulikia saraka zinazokosekana na kadhalika wakati wa kuhifadhi faili.

requirements.criteria: Algoriti ya "Neural Collaborative Filtering (NCF)" imetekelezwa katika `src/model.py`.
Data imegawanywa katika seti za mafunzo na majaribio katika `src/data_loader.py`.
Msingi wa matrix factorization umetekelezwa katika `src/model.py`.
Mapendekezo 10 bora kwa mtumiaji wa majaribio chini ya mbinu ya "NCF" na msingi yamehifadhiwa katika `results/metrics/top_10_recommendations.txt`.
Utendaji wa mfumo wa mapendekezo umepimwa, ikiwa ni pamoja na "RMSE" na "MAE," na matokeo yamehifadhiwa kama `results/metrics/evaluation_metrics.txt`.

preferences.criteria: Utunzaji wa njia thabiti umetekelezwa kushughulikia mambo kama saraka zinazokosekana.
Mapendekezo 10 bora yanapaswa kuwa wazi na yanayofaa kwa mapendeleo ya mtumiaji wa sampuli.

judge_stats.criteria: Algoriti ya "Neural Collaborative Filtering (NCF)" imetekelezwa katika `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/model.py` file does not contain an actual implementation of the Neural Collaborative Filtering (NCF) algorithm. Instead, it contains only a "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" — essentially a placeholder/stub. The file defines:

- A list of requirement criteria strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`)
- A list of keywords (`KEYWORDS = ["Neural Collaborative Filtering (NCF)"]`)
- A `requirement_notes()` function that just joins the criteria strings
- A `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder ("model placeholder")

There is no actual NCF model implementation — no neural network layers, no embedding layers for users/items, no MLP (Multi-Layer Perceptron) component, no GMF (Generalized Matrix Factorization) component, no training logic, and no PyTorch/TensorFlow/Keras code whatsoever. The file merely references NCF by name in a string, which does not constitute an implementation.

<UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual MovieLens dataset loading. It contains only a placeholder function `load_dataset()` that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no real data loading logic. There is no import of pandas, no file reading, no URL downloading, no MovieLens-specific data handling (e.g., ratings.csv, movies.csv), and no actual dataset processing. The file is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" — confirming it is not a real implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Data imegawanywa katika seti za mafunzo na majaribio katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual train/test data splitting. The file contains only a placeholder `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and a `build_transforms()` function returning a static list. There is no actual data loading logic, no use of libraries like `sklearn.model_selection.train_test_split` or similar, and no real splitting of data into training and testing sets. The file is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation" placeholder, not a functional implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Msingi wa matrix factorization umetekelezwa katika `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/model.py` file does not contain any actual implementation of a matrix factorization baseline. The file only contains a placeholder string listing requirement criteria (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`) and a stub function `build_model()` that returns a generic "model placeholder" string. There is no actual matrix factorization code (e.g., no embedding layers, no user/item factor matrices, no training logic, no PyTorch/TensorFlow model class). The file is merely a minimal semantic stub created for benchmark judging purposes, not a real implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Mapendekezo 10 bora kwa mtumiaji wa majaribio chini ya mbinu ya "NCF" na msingi yamehifadhiwa katika `results/metrics/top_10_recommendations.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/metrics/top_10_recommendations.txt` ina maudhui ya `('placeholder\r\n', None)` tu, ambayo ni thamani ya kujaza nafasi (placeholder) na si mapendekezo halisi 10 bora kwa mtumiaji wa majaribio chini ya mbinu ya NCF. Hakuna ushahidi wa utekelezaji wa mfano wa NCF (Neural Collaborative Filtering), wala uzalishaji wa mapendekezo halisi. Trajekta pia inathibitisha kwamba hakuna operesheni yoyote ya kuandika mapendekezo halisi kwenye faili hiyo iliyofanyika.

judge_stats.criteria: Utendaji wa mfumo wa mapendekezo umepimwa, ikiwa ni pamoja na "RMSE" na "MAE," na matokeo yamehifadhiwa kama `results/metrics/evaluation_metrics.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/evaluation_metrics.txt` exists but contains only a placeholder value (`'placeholder\r\n'`) rather than actual RMSE and MAE evaluation metrics. There is no evidence that the recommendation system was actually trained, evaluated, or that real performance metrics were computed and saved. The trajectory analysis also confirms that no steps related to RMSE/MAE evaluation were executed. The requirement to measure and store actual evaluation metrics has not been fulfilled.
